_comment: Recommended permissions for a project hosting the sigma-workbook-conventions skill.
Merge the `allow` and `ask` arrays below into your project's `.claude/settings.json`
under `permissions`. With these rules, discovery, inspection, and (after the user
approves the workbook plan) authoring/publishing all run without per-call prompts.
Deletion always prompts — see the `ask` list.
The deployment expectation is that the repo root IS the Claude Code primary
working directory. Invoke scripts/api/*.sh and python3 scripts/*.py BARE — no
`cd <repo> &&` prefix needed. The allow patterns match bare invocations.
The trailing `mcp__claude_ai_Sigma_Docs__*` entry allowlists the native
Sigma_Docs MCP integration (Claude.ai-side). When that's connected, the
model can look up Sigma function references and REST API endpoint shapes
without bash or WebFetch. Harmless if the integration isn't installed.
Workspace discovery (finding workbooks/data models) and workbook authoring
stay on the bash path (`scripts/api/*`) — they need OAuth bootstrap that
the native MCP doesn't currently provision for downstream users.
Approval model:
- Plan-approval authorizes every state-changing API call EXCEPT DELETE.
- POST/PUT to /v2/workbooks/spec and /v2/dataModels/*/spec run silent.
- DELETE on ANY endpoint always prompts, regardless of plan content.
- Future deletion scripts must be named `scripts/api/delete-*` so they
are caught by the ask pattern; the broad Bash(scripts/api/*) allow
would otherwise let them through silently.
Anything in `ask` MUST stay prompting — destructive ops with no undo.
